rulings.Case Title: GLENNA KINDLEY ET AL VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Dco Llc Fka Dana Companies Llc'S Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Dr. Smith'S Paper, And For Costs

DEFENDANT MCCORD CORPORATION'S (FKA MCCORD GASKET CORPORATION) Joinder To Defendant DCo Llc f/k/a Dana Companies Llc's Motion To Compel Dr. Smith's Paper, And For Costs. rulings.Case Title: JOHN SIGG ET AL VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 18, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Defendant's Application for Pro Hac Vice Admission of Michael F. Barzee as Counsel for Defendant TMS, Inc. is GRANTED under C.R.C. rule 9.40. No opposition filed. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on June 18, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: DORISANN WILLIAMS VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 18, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Maintaining the Current Trial Date with Preference is DENIED. Plaintiff's trial is set for September 9, 2024. Plaintiff failed to show that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing the party's interest in the litigation. (CCP section 36(a).) Plaintiff does not suggest that she will not be able to participate at her September trial. Rather, Plaintiff's concern is that "non-preference asbestos cases have generally not been assigned a courtroom on the initial trial date." To quell Plaintiff's concern, the Court shall make every effort and coordinate with dept. 206 to make sure the case is assigned to a courtroom on September 9, 2024. Plaintiff shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on June 18, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: ARNOLD BECK ET AL VS. BAYER CROPSCIENCE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 18, 2024, in Department 301, Line 3. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith on the newly added defendants: (i) STANDARD MOTOR PRODUCTS, INC.; (ii) PNEUMO ABEX LLC; (iii) EIDP, INC.; (iv) EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION; and (v) TOSCO CORPORATION, as well as on those defendants who have not yet appeared in the case. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all other Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on June 18, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)
